sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Fulham have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins but suffering 2 losses, including a high-scoring 4-5 defeat to Manchester City. Crystal Palace, on the other hand, have been more consistent with 2 wins, 2 losses, and a draw, highlighted by a 2-0 victory over Wolverhampton. Palace's ability to score late and win first halves in recent games gives them a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Crystal Palace have dominated Fulham, winning both matches with clean sheets (0-3 and 0-2). Fulham struggled to convert chances despite higher possession, while Palace were clinical with their opportunities. This trend suggests Palace might have the upper hand again.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fulham, sitting 15th with 17 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 22 goals, while their attack has managed 19 goals. Crystal Palace, positioned 6th with 23 points, boast a tighter defense (11 goals conceded) and a balanced attack (18 goals). Palace's superior league standing and defensive solidity make them favorites.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Fulham, Alex Iwobi (7.06 rating, 2 goals, 2 assists) and Harry Wilson (3 goals) could be pivotal in breaking down Palace's defense. Crystal Palace's Jean-Philippe Mateta (7 goals, 3rd in league scoring) remains a significant threat upfront, supported by Adam Wharton's creative play (5 big chances created).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Crystal Palace's recent head-to-head dominance, better league form, and defensive strength, they are likely to edge out Fulham. Sofascore's prediction of a 49% away win probability aligns with this analysis, though Fulham's home advantage at Craven Cottage could make it close. Expect a low-scoring affair based on historical trends of under 2.5 goals in their matchups.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Fulham and Crystal Palace at Craven Cottage ended in a 1-1 draw, setting the stage for an intriguing second half. Both teams showed moments of quality, with Edward Nketiah opening the scoring for Crystal Palace in the 20th minute, followed by a well-taken equalizer from Harry Wilson for Fulham in the 38th minute. The game has been competitive, with Fulham dominating possession and creating more opportunities, while Crystal Palace have been clinical on the counter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Fulham have controlled the ball for a significant portion of the half, showcasing their ability to build play through the midfield and into the final third. Their high number of corner kicks and penalty area entries reflect a persistent threat in attack, though they missed a key chance to take the lead. Defensively, they've been strong in duels, particularly in the air, which has limited Crystal Palace's set-piece threats. On the other hand, Crystal Palace have been more direct, making the most of their limited possession with a well-executed goal. Despite fewer entries into the attacking third, their efficiency in front of goal has kept them in the game, though their lower duel success rate suggests vulnerability when under pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at the break
halftimeSections.text: For Fulham, Harry Wilson has been the standout performer with his goal, demonstrating composure and sharpness in the final third. Alex Iwobi has also been influential, contributing to the attack with key passes and maintaining a high level of involvement. On the Crystal Palace side, Edward Nketiah's goal has given them confidence, while Adam Wharton’s assist highlights his vision and ability to unlock defenses. These players will likely be pivotal in determining the outcome in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides, with Crystal Palace starting brightly and pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, Fulham quickly took control, enjoying prolonged spells of attacking pressure through the middle of the half. There were brief moments where Palace regained some impetus, especially around their goal, but Fulham largely dictated the tempo as the half progressed, mounting consistent waves of attack towards the end. This suggests Fulham have the upper hand going into the break, but Palace have shown they can strike when given the chance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Fulham's approach has been to dominate possession and exploit width, evident from their frequent final third entries and corner opportunities. Their high pressing game has often caught Palace off guard, though they need to be more clinical with their chances. Crystal Palace, meanwhile, have relied on quick transitions and direct play, capitalizing on limited opportunities to score. Their defensive structure has held firm under pressure, but their lack of aerial dominance could be a concern if Fulham continue to target set pieces.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given Fulham's dominance in possession and attacking intent, they are likely to push for a winning goal in the second half, especially if they maintain their intensity. Crystal Palace will probably look to sit deeper and exploit counter-attacking opportunities, relying on players like Nketiah to make the difference. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, with Fulham possibly introducing fresh legs in midfield to sustain their pressing, while Palace might reinforce their defense to protect the point or seek a late winner.
